programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Bob Mortimer, Gabby Logan, Katherine Parkinson and Steve Backshall to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Rimmer finally tires of being embarrassed by his fellow crewmen, and decides to take his revenge by forcing them to spend 12 weeks in strict quarantine after they are exposed to a deadly virus. Sci-fi comedy, starring Craig Charles and Chris Barrie. Originally broadcast in 1992

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: An experiment with a machine that can make duplicates of an object goes wrong and crew members are aghast to encounter their evil 'doppelgangers'

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Starbug crashes while trying to evade a giant squid, but instead of dying the crew members discover all their memories of their time on Red Dwarf had been generated by a video game - one they had been playing extremely badly. Sci-fi comedy, starring Craig Charles, Chris Barrie, Danny John-Jules and Robert Llewellyn, with guest star Timothy Spall

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of guests Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Milton Jones, Zoe Lyons and Geoff Norcott. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy takes the kids away to her parents while Lee stays in the house to oversee the building work being done their kitchen, but unfortunately the tradesman he has employed has an annoyingly relaxed attitude to deadlines. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan hosts an extended episode of the satirical current affairs quiz, with team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton joined by comedy writer Andy Hamilton and comedian Chloe Petts

programme.title: Mel Giedroyc: Unforgivable
programme.desc: Mel and Lou Sanders are joined by Nish Kumar, Ruby Wax and Reuben Kaye as Nish spills the beans on X-rated workplace behaviour and Ruby tells of a drug-smuggling past

programme.title: Mel Giedroyc: Unforgivable
programme.desc: Mel and Lou are joined by Rafe Spall, Carol Vorderman and Dan Tiernan as Rafe lifts the lid on how he evacuated a swimming pool and Carol reveals how she ruined a wedding

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Tinker and Lovejoy set out to unravel the meaning behind a strange carving of a Japanese firefly cage, helped by Lady Jane Felsham. Drama, starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey are suspected of carrying out a spate of burglaries in Chigwell and snooty man-eater Dorien sees her chance to get rid of the neighbourhood's low-life element once and for all by not speaking up in their defence. With Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Still reeling from the revelation about Alun and with Monica exonerated, the spotlight of suspicion falls upon Nicola. Bergerac is left with his career on the line

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Conway is thrilled to meet a pop idol from his youth, but fails to anticipate the trouble heading his way

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Mike comes to the rescue of a child maths prodigy whose drink has been spiked by the jealous boyfriend of a fellow student. Julia asks Nick to compete in a charity wheelchair race and Michelle is furious when Mike is late for a date

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ronnie returns home to find George has organised a Roman-themed surprise for his 40th birthday. Michelle reveals to Ruth her plans to offer a permanent contract to Mike, and Melody is caught in an awkward position when an irate husband objects to his wife receiving breast reduction surgery - insisting she is fine the way she is

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: On a busy night behind the bar, Mickie notices Jodie is becoming breathless and feverish, and tries to persuade her to seek medical help. Tricia and Mark find working together tense, but agree to be as professional as possible, while Diane suffers from pre-wedding jitters and turns to Ric for support. Her fears may be well-founded, as Owen's stag night ends with him kissing Chrissie

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie welcomes two new nurses to the ED, but while Lloyd gets off to a flying start by saving the life of his first patient in resus, a nervous Scarlett seems convinced her skills lie more in flirting with consultants than in caring for people. Meanwhile, teaching assistant Harry (Lee Mead) is involved in an altercation between a senior colleague and a problem student

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Conway is thrilled to meet a pop idol from his youth, but fails to anticipate the trouble heading his way

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The grieving of the Trueman/Fox family takes an unexpected turn, Phil makes a significant decision regarding Louise, and Darren follows the path of true love

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: As the Square's residents reel from the previous night's events, Libby and Chelsea's differences threaten to tear their family apart. Meanwhile, Phil and Peggy pay Ben a visit

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.desc: Recovering addict Zoe is abducted only to wake up in a forest wearing a vintage ballgown. Terrified that her unknown assallant will come back for her, her path leads to Harry and Fergus. But when she is kidnapped a second time, the pair put themselves in mortal danger to save her life

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: A headmaster is found dead in his office, and Blake's subsequent investigation stirs up some demons from the past - particularly for chief of police Matthew Lawson, who was once a pupil at the school. Starring Craig McLachlan and Joel Tobeck

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: An overzealous taxman sets out to snare Lovejoy - not to mention an axe-wielding jailbird determined to retrieve an ornate Arab head-dress. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien's husband goes missing, and she's later seen digging a suspiciously man-shaped hole in the garden, prompting Sharon and Tracey to fear their neighbour has taken to murder. Lesley Joseph stars, with Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Marina wants to deliver a giant cuddly panda as her birthday present to lover Howard, so ropes in the reluctant trio to help. Comedy, featuring a guest appearance by comedian Jim Bowen and starring Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Desperate to earn some cash, Compo reveals a musical talent after being endorsed by several artists in the area and sets out as a one-man band. Comedy, starring Bill Owen, Brian Wilde and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy finds an interest in camcorders, much to the annoyance of some country ramblers. When later playing his footage back on Pearl's video machine, it becomes apparent the lens has inadvertently caught her and Howard in a compromising situation. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man suspected of killing a police officer is seen near Kembleford and Mallory is determined to catch him, but he has to ask the sleuthing priest for help when disaster strikes

programme.title: Happy Valley
programme.desc: Daniel suspects somebody at school might be responsible for Ryan's new-found interest in Tommy, and tries to explain that he wasn't really a dad to him. Despite Sean protesting his innocence, Jodie and John receive permission to charge him with all of the murders

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The ex-coppers find themselves under the microscope when a psychologist arrives to conduct a study into older men in the workplace - but she soon reveals a more personal reason for her visit. It turns out that five years ago her brother, a cycle courier, was the victim of a hit-and-run that left him unable to recall anything prior to the incident. Now he says he was targeted for the parcel he was carrying, which was stolen at the scene - or so he believes. Sally Phillips guest stars

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A priest is murdered in a tiny village, the site of a hugely publicised miracle cure a few weeks earlier, and the crime-solving duo arrive to find a community deeply divided and steeped in superstition. Searching for a likely suspect, they instead discover the body of a girl killed 40 years previously. James Bolam, Lindsey Coulson and Roger Lloyd-Pack guest star, with Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: An overzealous taxman sets out to snare Lovejoy - not to mention an axe-wielding jailbird determined to retrieve an ornate Arab head-dress. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.desc: The literary influenced killing of a heckler during a guided tour brings Harry and Fergus into the middle of a literature-themed murder plot

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kingdom
programme.desc: Angela and Tony Forshaw want to sue for damages when their daughter Laura is refused a place at Cambridge. Peter discovers why Gloria and her son Scott are acting strangely, and Snell takes on the gas company when they threaten to build on sheep-grazing land. Meanwhile, more information comes to light about Simon's lifestyle

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien's husband goes missing, and she's later seen digging a suspiciously man-shaped hole in the garden, prompting Sharon and Tracey to fear their neighbour has taken to murder. Lesley Joseph stars, with Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon's husband Chris sees a family tragedy as the perfect opportunity to escape from prison. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Trouble lies in store for Sharon when she finds romance, and Tracey starts a business venture with Dorien. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.sub-title: Three Men and a Brittle Lady
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer offers to track down a conman after Charlie is swindled out of a china figurine. He also helps Charlotte find the thieves who've robbed her house. Drama, guest starring Lionel Jeffries and Eve Matheson

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dealer falls under the suspicion of an ambitious and ruthless police officer, who suspects he is handling stolen European furniture. Meanwhile, Tinker joins Eric for a brief holiday in Essex, where they soon become involved in a smuggling racket. Warren Mitchell and Reece Dinsdale guest star, with Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer whisks Charlotte away for a weekend in Wales, but before long they find themselves hunting for a murderer and a rare Celtic cross. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Caroline Langrishe

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man suspected of killing a police officer is seen near Kembleford and Mallory is determined to catch him, but he has to ask the sleuthing priest for help when disaster strikes

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The body of a missing computer expert turns up in a hospital morgue under a false name, so the team reopens the investigation into his disappearance. The dead man worked for the Metropolitan Police, giving him access to a great deal of sensitive information - added to which, he was in contact with a group of online hackers. But he died of natural causes - so why was his body hidden, and by who? As the seasoned detectives unravel the mystery, Brian struggles to accept Steve as the new Ucos member, forcing Esther to intervene. Guest starring Sarah Smart and Alex Jennings

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The death of a teenage tennis star occupies the team when it is suggested her suicide jump two years earlier was anything but. They soon find several people with motives for her murder, including her coach Nick and agent Anthony, but it seems the one with the most to benefit from the girl's demise was her great rival Fawn Brammall - a shy and subdued player who proves difficult to question. Meanwhile, Gerry wonders if he did enough to encourage his own daughter's sporting ambitions and Brian hatches a plan to turn his dog into a movie star. Detective drama, guest starring Tamzin Outhwaite and Alexei Sayle

programme.title: Signora Volpe
programme.desc: Sylvia is abducted by an adversary from her past, and Riva and her family are forced to heal long-standing grudges and work together to find her

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Shepherd and his team investigate when Peggy Froome, a devoted fan of the '80s band Stolen Arrow, falls to her death from a bell tower. The detective must dig into the band's secrets and dark history to find the actual cause of the victim's demise. Neill Rea stars

programme.title: Monsieur Spade
programme.desc: The tragedy of the horrific murders are felt by all in the town. Pulled out of retirement, Spade begins to investigate, while Teresa keeps her cards close to her chest

programme.title: The Last Detective
programme.desc: Dangerous investigates the disappearance of a man whose wife seems strangely unconcerned about his whereabouts, and two ballroom-dancing regulars discover a tragic secret. Julia McKenzie and Sian Phillips guest star, with Peter Davison and Sean Hughes

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The body of a missing computer expert turns up in a hospital morgue under a false name, so the team reopens the investigation into his disappearance. The dead man worked for the Metropolitan Police, giving him access to a great deal of sensitive information - added to which, he was in contact with a group of online hackers. But he died of natural causes - so why was his body hidden, and by who? As the seasoned detectives unravel the mystery, Brian struggles to accept Steve as the new Ucos member, forcing Esther to intervene. Guest starring Sarah Smart and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is drawn into a conflict between an estranged mother and daughter, both of whom are pregnant. Nurse Crane visits a photographic gallery in Soho with Sgt Woolf, but the trip does not go to plan, and Sister Frances' shyness causes concern. Trixie's keep fit class is chosen to host a special event, but her star pupil is devastated to learn that she is pregnant

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: TV host Prunella Gladwell comes to St Vincent's Convent to present an episode of her top-rated cookery show involving six contestants, including Sister Boniface, competing for a cash prize. Contestant Gladys Tibbs dies during the recording and Sister Boniface determines she was poisoned by belladonna berries added to her own cake - a cake that should have been tasted by Gladwell

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Members of an all-women motorcycle club arrive in Brokenwood for the wedding of one of their number, but a murder casts suspicion among their ranks and throws the town into chaos. Detective drama set in a New Zealand town, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Adrian is put through the wringer when he attempts to apply for social benefits and Aveline reveals she has met a nice boy in London

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Freddie Boswell returns home, shocking Nellie with his new perspective on life. Julie and Aveline create chaos when they each announce they are leaving, while Adrian decides that Carmen is driven too much by pure lust rather than wanting to develop a proper relationship

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing America's decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitler's declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The battle for North Africa, which involved three years of fighting between Rommel's Afrika Korps and Montgomery's Desert Rats and culminated in the Allied victory at El Alamein. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: An account of Hitler's decision to invade the Soviet Union, which produced an initial series of dazzling victories until the harsh winter of 1941 took hold, with fatal consequences for the ill-equipped German army. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates this documentary chronicling events leading up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbour on December 7, 1941, and the subsequent collapse of unprepared British forces before the advancing enemy in Singapore. Includes interviews with veterans from both sides

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing America's decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitler's declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Blind adventurer Amar Latif travels along one of the region's most stunning river valleys, abseiling into a hidden gorge, canoeing across a reservoir, and meeting the local llamas while following a riverside trail along the Nidd

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simon's journey begins at the Giant's Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwell's influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: A journey around the region, examining the challenges currently facing the nations in the area. Simon begins in Malta, uncovering the country's reputation for organised crime, before moving on to the southern coast of Italy, where he joins police officers exploring underground tunnels used by crime bosses on the run. Finally, he arrives in Albania and discovers an ancient tradition of revenge where children can pay the price for their parents' actions

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: Life in the treacherous seas that surround the South American wilderness. The 4,000-mile coast stretches from the cold waters of Cape Horn - where rockhopper penguins overcome huge challenges to raise their young - to the far north's much warmer climate, where huge elephant seals battle for position in the heat of the desert. Orcas ram-raid the beaches, grabbing seal pups to feed their young, and fishermen are also incentivised to gather the sea's bounty. Narrated by Santiago Cabrera

programme.desc: A journey around the region, examining the challenges currently facing the nations in the area. Simon begins in Malta, uncovering the country's reputation for organised crime, before moving on to the southern coast of Italy, where he joins police officers exploring underground tunnels used by crime bosses on the run. Finally, he arrives in Albania and discovers an ancient tradition of revenge where children can pay the price for their parents' actions

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simon's journey begins at the Giant's Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwell's influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: A journey around the region, examining the challenges currently facing the nations in the area. Simon begins in Malta, uncovering the country's reputation for organised crime, before moving on to the southern coast of Italy, where he joins police officers exploring underground tunnels used by crime bosses on the run. Finally, he arrives in Albania and discovers an ancient tradition of revenge where children can pay the price for their parents' actions

programme.title: Open All Hours

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: David Jason returns as Granville in this follow-up to Roy Clarke's popular comedy Open All Hours, as he re-opens the doors of the nation's favourite corner shop. In the opening episode, Arkwright's protege finds an old mangle in the yard - and wonders how he can best put it to use. Meanwhile, Beth continues to pressure Leroy to find a date for her best friend, Ruby, who has been suspended at work. Elsewhere, Eric and Cyril tutor Mr Newbold in the art of romance - and how to get closer to Mrs Rossi. Co-starring James Baxter, Johnny Vegas and Katie Redford

programme.title: The Power of Parker
programme.desc: Comedy about a self-made man struggling to keep his head above water as the 1980s turn into the 1990s. Martin Parker has the ambition of Alan Sugar and the swagger of Robert Kilroy Silk. With a chain of electrical stores bearing his name, he seems to have it all. However the truth is he swimming in debt, his complicated private life is catching up with him and he's starting to lose his power. Written by and starring Sian Gibson, with Conleth Hill, Rosie Cavaliero, George Costigan, Sheila Reid and Jason Barnett

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Miserly shopkeeper Arkwright organises a sales drive in a bid to boost profits - and rid himself of the vast consignment of apples he has mistakenly ordered. An unhappy Granville is persuaded to demonstrate the merchandise. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: David Jason returns as Granville in this follow-up to Roy Clarke's popular comedy Open All Hours, as he re-opens the doors of the nation's favourite corner shop. In the opening episode, Arkwright's protege finds an old mangle in the yard - and wonders how he can best put it to use. Meanwhile, Beth continues to pressure Leroy to find a date for her best friend, Ruby, who has been suspended at work. Elsewhere, Eric and Cyril tutor Mr Newbold in the art of romance - and how to get closer to Mrs Rossi. Co-starring James Baxter, Johnny Vegas and Katie Redford

The nights are both very different, but Courtney's ends with a shock revelation as Dan makes a decision about her dream job. Comedy, guest starring Paula Wilcox, with Romesh Ranganathan, Jessica Knappett and Aisling Bea

programme.title: Avoidance
programme.desc: It's the day of Dan's baby shower and Jonathan tries to end things with Megan before Claire finds out - only for a dead cat to foil his plan. The reality of having a baby alongside Dan's new job sends Courtney spiralling. Comedy, with Romesh Ranganathan and Jessica Knappett

programme.title: Avoidance
programme.desc: Dan and Courtney's relaxing weekend away is interrupted when she goes into labour and Jonathan gets a phone call asking to drive them to hospital. Keen to make it up to Claire, Jonathan asks her to come along too - but her suitcase is already packed for her trip to Venice with Brett. Meanwhile, Spencer makes his own romantic gesture. Comedy, with Romesh Ranganathan and Jessica Knappett

programme.title: The Power of Parker
programme.desc: Comedy about a self-made man struggling to keep his head above water as the 1980s turn into the 1990s. Martin Parker has the ambition of Alan Sugar and the swagger of Robert Kilroy Silk. With a chain of electrical stores bearing his name, he seems to have it all. However the truth is he swimming in debt, his complicated private life is catching up with him and he's starting to lose his power. Written by and starring Sian Gibson, with Conleth Hill, Rosie Cavaliero, George Costigan, Sheila Reid and Jason Barnett

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony decides to break up with his girlfriend and agonises over the best way to go about it. He unintentionally creates the ideal opportunity by cancelling a date to prepare a meal for Deborah. Starring Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.sub-title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Cash-strapped Alison and Mike think all their problems have been solved when they inherit a grand country house, unaware that it is falling apart and teeming with the ghosts of former inhabitants. The spooks must act immediately to halt the new owners' plans to turn the house into a hotel, but their attempts to scare them away fail miserably. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe, Mathew Baynton, Simon Farnaby, Martha Howe-Douglas, Jim Howick, Laurence Rickard and Ben Willbond

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison finds herself hounded by the ghosts, who now realise she can both see and hear them, and tries to ignore their existence - a tactic which only exacerbates the problem when the Captain puts the ghosts onto a war footing. Meanwhile, Mike's attempts to turn the house into a home leads to a DIY disaster, saddling Lady Button with an ill-suited roommate, and Julian learns about his questionable legacy when the spooks discover the internet. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Lies, Deceit and Betrayal
programme.desc: The extraordinary case of Ian Paterson, a breast surgeon who subjected more than 1,000 of his patients to needless and damaging surgery

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Four years after the Daniels family moved into their home there are still moving boxes left unopened in the garage leaving little room for the family of five. Stacey, with the help of organiser Dilly, carpenter Rob and cleaner Iwan, puts all their belongings on display in a warehouse for them to see just how much unnecessary clutter they have including 492 DVDs, 35 happy birthday banners, 12 unopened chandeliers and 905 items of baby clothing

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Louis spends time with the global pop star Rita Ora. Louis meets Rita at her dad's pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her family's arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. Later, Louis spends time with Rita on the set of her latest music video in LA, before joining her as she puts on a spectacular live performance in Albania

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Property Brothers: Forever Homes
programme.desc: The Scott brothers renovate a beachside home in Marina del Ray for owners Tammy and Dimiter

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, his team and a group of volunteers are in Monmouth, Wales, to transform the family home of Charlotte, a mother recovering from a stroke that left her with brain damage, speech loss and partial paralysis. The current layout of her home makes it difficult for Charlotte to move around and have her physiotherapy treatment, so the team redesign the house to make things easier for her, her husband Rob and their children

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to the Isle of Sheppey in Kent to meet two extraordinary dads, Garry and Kyle, who have adopted four vulnerable children - three of whom have special needs - to give them the best life possible. But their cramped three-bedroom bungalow is making family life difficult. So to give this family the space they so desperately need, the DIY SOS builders are undertaking their most ambitious project yet - to demolish the bungalow and build a bespoke five-bedroom house in its place. But with just nine days to complete the task, it looks like being their toughest one yet

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets midwife and kidney patient Sascha, who has to undergo intense dialysis sessions for hours on end, five days a week. This places the mother-of-four's body under great stress and the constant threat of blood clots gives her a life expectancy of just a few years. Determined to help, the DIY SOS team, bolstered by the local tradespeople and volunteers of Welwyn Garden City, renovate her home to include a dedicated dialysis room, offering her the chance of many more years with her family

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A 10-month-old puppy called Albie is knocked over by a car on a family camping holiday is rushed in as an emergency admission, and Noel Fitzpatrick fears this may be a life or death situation. Elsewhere, a Scottish terrier has a mysterious ailment, and a 16-month-old spaniel, is suffering from severe arthritis affecting both hips

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick faces one of his greatest practical and ethical challenges yet when he's introduced to Hermes, a tortoise with only one good leg. Can he provide Hermes with three prosthetic feet, using tiny titanium implants? Meanwhile, a rottweiler called Dave urgently needs an upgrade to his previous hip replacements

programme.desc: Pete helps rehabilitate a group of ex-racing greyhounds who have never experienced life beyond the racetrack

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Staffy Boxer Cross Crumble, who's been at the centre in Basildon for over three years, stuck without a foster or permanent home in sight

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Pat's family make their annual pilgrimage to the site of his death and he begs Alison to pass on a message from the other side - a plan complicated when he realises a shocking truth about his marriage. Meanwhile, Mike struggles to come to terms with his bathroom-based paranoia, and Kitty tries to find out where babies come from. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: First edition of the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedian as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, including a woman frightened of her own shadow, the wife of a cannibal on death row, a couple with a unique approach to parenting, an Irish nurse who will not let patients come between her and her twin passions, and a teenager who would rather be right than happy. From February 2004

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Four years after the Daniels family moved into their home there are still moving boxes left unopened in the garage leaving little room for the family of five. Stacey, with the help of organiser Dilly, carpenter Rob and cleaner Iwan, puts all their belongings on display in a warehouse for them to see just how much unnecessary clutter they have including 492 DVDs, 35 happy birthday banners, 12 unopened chandeliers and 905 items of baby clothing

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Louis spends time with the global pop star Rita Ora. Louis meets Rita at her dad's pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her family's arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. Later, Louis spends time with Rita on the set of her latest music video in LA, before joining her as she puts on a spectacular live performance in Albania

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Property Brothers: Forever Homes
programme.desc: The Scott brothers renovate a beachside home in Marina del Ray for owners Tammy and Dimiter

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, his team and a group of volunteers are in Monmouth, Wales, to transform the family home of Charlotte, a mother recovering from a stroke that left her with brain damage, speech loss and partial paralysis. The current layout of her home makes it difficult for Charlotte to move around and have her physiotherapy treatment, so the team redesign the house to make things easier for her, her husband Rob and their children

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to the Isle of Sheppey in Kent to meet two extraordinary dads, Garry and Kyle, who have adopted four vulnerable children - three of whom have special needs - to give them the best life possible. But their cramped three-bedroom bungalow is making family life difficult. So to give this family the space they so desperately need, the DIY SOS builders are undertaking their most ambitious project yet - to demolish the bungalow and build a bespoke five-bedroom house in its place. But with just nine days to complete the task, it looks like being their toughest one yet

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets midwife and kidney patient Sascha, who has to undergo intense dialysis sessions for hours on end, five days a week. This places the mother-of-four's body under great stress and the constant threat of blood clots gives her a life expectancy of just a few years. Determined to help, the DIY SOS team, bolstered by the local tradespeople and volunteers of Welwyn Garden City, renovate her home to include a dedicated dialysis room, offering her the chance of many more years with her family

programme.title: The Supervet

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick faces one of his greatest practical and ethical challenges yet when he's introduced to Hermes, a tortoise with only one good leg. programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: First edition of the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedian as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, including a woman frightened of her own shadow, the wife of a cannibal on death row, a couple with a unique approach to parenting, an Irish nurse who will not let patients come between her and her twin passions, and a teenager who would rather be right than happy. From February 2004

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: True-crime series hosted by Below Deck fan favourite Captain Lee Rosbach, who uses his nautical expertise to explore chilling murders that have happened on lakes, rivers and seas. When a Bahamas-bound boat is found abandoned 140 miles off course, investigators wade through blood evidence and reports of a possible pirate attack as they look for answers

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Three Swedish Death Cleaners - an organizer, a designer and a psychologist - travel across America to help people face mortality and organise their homes and lives before it's too late. programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough has a passion for birds' eggs, which protect the young from the outside world at the same time as allowing them to breathe and are strong enough to withstand the full weight of an incubating parent and weak enough to allow a chick to break free. In this documentary, the broadcaster reveals the secrets of birds' eggs from creation to hatching, exploring how they are made, the reasons behind their shape and their intrinsic purpose

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will Trickett and Gus Gregory find an orange Reliant Kitten that an engineer has given up on - the body is detached from the chassis, it doesn't run and most of the bits it needs are in boxes in the boot. However, the guys are sure there's money to be made from it

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Wales, where they pick up and restore a dentist's table and a barber's chair, and make a coffee table out of parquet flooring

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: The crew of Karen of Ladram hunts hake off Cornwall, but the trip is thrown into jeopardy when a crew member's health deteriorates and their catch is decimated when seals get to the fish first. Elsewhere, a rookie crew works in the Irish Sea, trainee skipper Michael steps up in a storm, and Tydus heads out in search of crabs

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: After 14 decades centre stage the Manchester Town Hall is in urgent need of repair. Now a dedicated team are working hard to do this architectural giant justice

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: The work to upgrade London's Victorian sewer system, follow machines as they bore a network of tunnels linking Bazalgette's old sewer network to the Tideway Super-Sewer

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A 1970s Bond Bug three-wheeler divides opinion among Mathewsons customers, while a retired engineer reluctantly auctions off his late father's treasured 60s Jaguar Mark 2

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Mathewsons agree to sell a vintage threshing machine and large collection of tractors, but extracting them from old barns on a Yorkshire farm proves a bigger job than anticipated

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The history of the concentration camp, charting its origins as a place to hold political prisoners through its development into a mechanised factory for mass murder. The first episode features the accounts of Polish inmates held there prior to the Holocaust, Jewish survivors and a former SS officer, who discusses being placed in charge of a firing squad

programme.title: Ghosts of Alderney: Hitler's Island Slaves
programme.desc: Artist Piers Secunda uncovers the hidden history of Alderney to reveal new evidence of atrocities committed by the Nazis on this tiny island during the Second World War. He examines how slave workers from across Europe were shipped to Alderney to build a fortress for Hitler

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team set up their stalls at Dulwich Art Gallery for the first of two programmes from south London. Items brought in for valuation include one of the most valuable paintings seen on the programme in recent years, a little-known photograph of Winston Churchill and a costume dating from the earliest days of pantomime

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second of two programmes from Dulwich Picture Gallery in south London. Items brought in for appraisal by the experts include an early illustration by Heath Robinson, a rare set of miniatures found on a bus, and a book that has been on loan for half a century

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave goes to Wales to bag a Sierra Cosworth, a sought-after car in the bidding market. Dad Derek gets his hands on a more sedate 1926 Standard Tourer - a quirky little motor

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Suffolk to collect two Bentleys with a majestic pedigree; Cynthia a stunning 1963 S3 and Harry, a 1965 Continental

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The history of the concentration camp, charting its origins as a place to hold political prisoners through its development into a mechanised factory for mass murder. programme.title: Ghosts of Alderney: Hitler's Island Slaves
programme.desc: Artist Piers Secunda uncovers the hidden history of Alderney to reveal new evidence of atrocities committed by the Nazis on this tiny island during the Second World War. He examines how slave workers from across Europe were shipped to Alderney to build a fortress for Hitler

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: How air-breathing mammals have found remarkable ways to overcome the many challenges of a life in water, from freshwater jungle ponds to the dark depths of the open ocean. Featuring footage of the birth of a huge sperm whale calf, never-before-filmed orca hunting behaviour, and coastal coyotes in Mexico that have learnt they can benefit from the daily offerings washed up on the shore with each new tide

programme.title: Mammals

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: The work to upgrade London's Victorian sewer system, follow machines as they bore a network of tunnels linking Bazalgette's old sewer network to the Tideway Super-Sewer

programme.desc: A 1970s Bond Bug three-wheeler divides opinion among Mathewsons customers, while a retired engineer reluctantly auctions off his late father's treasured 60s Jaguar Mark 2

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The history of the concentration camp, charting its origins as a place to hold political prisoners through its development into a mechanised factory for mass murder. The first episode features the accounts of Polish inmates held there prior to the Holocaust, Jewish survivors and a former SS officer, who discusses being placed in charge of a firing squad

programme.title: Ghosts of Alderney: Hitler's Island Slaves
programme.desc: Artist Piers Secunda uncovers the hidden history of Alderney to reveal new evidence of atrocities committed by the Nazis on this tiny island during the Second World War. He examines how slave workers from across Europe were shipped to Alderney to build a fortress for Hitler

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team set up their stalls at Dulwich Art Gallery for the first of two programmes from south London. programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.desc: Part two of two. Cassie and Sam's long-awaited wedding has finally arrived. Sam receives some help from Grace in finding the Forever Tree. However, the wedding brings up complex emotions for Grace. Fantasy, starring Catherine Bell

programme.desc: Early morning chat show presented by Lorraine Kelly, with celebrity interviews, entertainment news, cooking tips, fashion features and musical guests.

programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The boxer talks about his life inside and outside the ring. From growing up as a triplet to when he became the first Irishman to win an Olympic Gold medal in boxing in 1992

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs - India
programme.desc: The comedian and dog-lover presents his final report from Delhi, where 400,000 pooches live on the streets. Paul joins in with the celebrations for Diwali, the Hindu festival of light, with the people and animals of one of Delhi's oldest dog shelters. He also helps a traumatised puppy make new friends, joins medics as they rush to rescue a four-week-old street pup who may have been hit by a car and falls in love with an elderly black labrador who lives at the rescue centre. Will he be able to resist bringing the pooch back to Britain with him?

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the jockey, who reflects on a pioneering career, a life immersed in horse racing, and her role as a role model to other women

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade visits the Parana River in Argentina to investigate the death of a girl thought to have been killed by a creature the locals call the 'river dog'. Armed with a razor-sharp blade tipped with tissue-destroying venom, the species lives 500 miles from the sea and proves to be the largest totally freshwater river monster Jeremy has ever caught

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Jason Manford presents the show which asks how well people really know their kids, by challenging parents to guess what their children will do in a variety of entertaining situations. Each show sees youngsters filmed while taking part in a variety of classroom activities and games, unaware that we're actually testing everything from empathy and lateral thinking to curiosity and rule breaking

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Charlotte, Dylan, Karen and Jay

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Opera singer and model Paige from Hertfordshire returns to the show to be dressed by three male suitors. Whose outfit will she choose to wear on the date?

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Healthcare co-ordinator Brad from Pontefract in West Yorkshire puts his faith in the fashion choices of Ashley, James and Mike

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder follows the investigation into the suspicious death of 30-year-old Lyndsey Vaux, whose body was found in the Wigan home she shared with her partner Becky Reid and Becky's mum Gillian. Neighbours told police that the victim suffered regular abuse and beatings at the hands of her girlfriend. But when the pathology report proved inconclusive, police needed to find proof that she was responsible

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: From a record-breaking skyscraper to one of the world's most ambitious mega roads, this landmark series returns to reveal the extraordinary innovations that enable engineers to build these remarkable superstructures, along with the inner secrets of how they work. Each episode charts the construction of one epic project from start to finish, with a mix of CGI animation, character-driven actuality, and bodycam, drone and time-lapse footage helping to break down the construction and transform our understanding of these incredible engineering wonders

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Michael, Donna, Louise and Andy pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.desc: Back on the Parsifal III in Sardinia, with his heads of departments returning for another season, Captain Glenn is poised for his smoothest sail yet

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Children are filmed in a school specially rigged with hidden cameras. The family with the most correct predictions goes through to the final. And the kids choose the prizes

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the Olympic boxing silver medallist, who discusses his journey to the top of the amateur boxing world and his hidden struggles with addiction

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the Gaelic football legend who grew up in The Troubles in Armagh, and recovered from a missed penalty to score the winning goal in their only All-Ireland win

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi comes to the rescue of more struggling hotels, beginning with a visit to Epstein House in Liverpool where owner Patrick Duggan is having problems keeping his business afloat. The property was the home of the Beatles' manager Brian Epstein and Patrick was sure the guest house would be highly profitable when he bought it, but such expectations have yet to be realised

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The race to build the world's next generation of engineering wonders - structures and machines that are more ambitious and more advanced than anything seen before

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A local nightclub owner is admitted with symptoms of poisoning, and his son is quick to point the finger of suspicion at his father's glamorous wife. Frankie is shocked to see Dr Ormerod doubt himself as they tend a drunken farmer after a tractor accident, while Dr Goodwin deals with a case of domestic abuse and a stationery salesman takes a shine to Lizzie. Robert Daws and Scott Taylor star

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Goodwin is accused of taking sides when he goes too far to help a woman who claims her husband is hitting her, and there is a mystery to solve when a patient is admitted under a false name. Meanwhile, Dr Ormerod's strange behaviour shows signs of becoming worse - and Frankie fears the surgeon is becoming addicted to his medication. Medical drama, with Robert Daws, Wendy Craig and Amy Robbins

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The concluding part of an interview with the former Republic of Ireland star, as he discusses with Tommy Martin the high esteem he was held in for club and country while facing battles on and off the pitch

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines an incident in 2003 where two girls were left fighting for their lives, having been caught in an exchange of gunfire between two rival gangs. The case had far-reaching implications, putting gun violence in Britain's inner cities at the top of the political agenda and changing how the courts protect key witnesses

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie and Sam's much overdue date doesn't go as planned, and they end up stranded on an island; Joy dreams about her parents. Catherine Bnell stars

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie and Sam's double date with hospital administrator Grant and his girlfriend Monica, doesn't go quite as planned. Martha organizes a Grease themed movie night in the park. Stars Catherine Bell and James Denton. The Party The Party Cassie and Sam's much overdue date doesn't go as planned and they end up stranded on an island. Joy dreams about her parents. Catherine Bell and James Denton star

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade ventures into Brazil's cattle country as he searches for a deadly electric eel that has killed three cowboys. To catch his elusive prey he has to combat all his fears, and after a long chase he finally corners it in an unexpected place

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. This time, Paul meets an energetic Staffie cross who needs help calming down in kennels, falls in love with a rare West African dog called an Azawakh, and coaxes an overbred bulldog to come out of its shell

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets more dogs struggling to find a home, including a golden retriever with an incontinence problem and a boisterous border collie with a bad hip - who needs Paul's help in the hydrotherapy centre. He also calls in on a tiny pregnant mongrel, and spends time with an OAP Patterdale who just needs a bit of company in her twilight years

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Children are filmed in a school specially rigged with hidden cameras. In the studio, host Jason Manford challenges parents to predict what their child will do in each situation, providing an insight into the developing minds of five to seven year-olds. The family with the most correct predictions goes through to the final. And the kids choose the prizes

programme.title: Genius Game
programme.desc: The seven remaining players take on the next Main Match, Safari Race, a game in which contestants must back the winning animals to gain points but they soon start to realise the importance of playing a social game as well as a strategic one

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing: LA
programme.desc: Flagg and Altman go head-to-head as they compete to sell their listings in Trousdale, and Tracy gets a sneak peek at a new Bel Air property.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Relationship-based reality show in which singletons seeking a romance compete in the hope of finding love - and winning a £50,000 prize

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Captain Glenn is hopeful that Colin's plan to rebuild the engine will work, but the guests tell him this is the worst trip ever and specifically blame Daisy's lack of service

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
programme.desc: The 10th series reunion comes to a close as Raquel faces her former friends and the group revisits the girls' trip to Las Vegas and Lake Havasu

programme.title: Dubai Hustle
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of young Brits making it in the world of luxury high-end property in Dubai

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan, Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty, Shaun 'The Dark Destroyer' Wallace, Mark 'The Beast' Labbett and Darragh 'The Menace' Ennis come together to take on more contestants from across the country. Can any of them come out on top? Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Out There
programme.desc: When Turuk arrives at the farm, Nathan confesses to killing Kenny, but Turuk knows that Johnny actually did the deed. Nathan pitches the next-door farm he's buying to Turuk as a new warehouse, but Crowther now has an eyewitness account of who burnt the car in the description of a teenage boy and two men

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: The net begins to close on Charles and Marie-Andree in India,and he hatches a daring plan to escape to South America, but he is becoming reckless and soon everything spirals out of control. In Bangkok, Herman, Angela and the Action Committee face up to life after the Sobhraj case

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: News at 12:30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team, followed by sport and weather

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants answer general knowledge questions and work as a team to take on one of the ruthless Chasers and secure a cash prize

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Oli, Ravi, Anya and Liz pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Early morning chat show presented by Lorraine Kelly, with celebrity interviews, entertainment news, cooking tips, fashion features and musical guests.

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: News at 7
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with Ross's Wedding - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Rachel and a heavily pregnant Phoebe stay at home while Chandler, Joey and Monica go to England for Ross's wedding to Emily. Once there, however, it seems all Joey and Chandler can do is bicker. Guest starring Richard Branson and Sarah Ferguson

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert investigates a noise in the barn while working on the farm and is attacked from behind and knocked unconscious, while Manpreet lets some news slip

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sam shows Roy a tracking device he found in his school bag, Cassie gets distracted while childminding, Kit and Lisa's investigation stalls, and Ben suffers a meltdown at the end of the week from hell

programme.title: The Snapper (1993)
programme.desc: A 20-year-old woman from a large family announces she is pregnant, but refuses to reveal the identity of the father. Her protective dad is determined to stand by her through thick and thin, but over the next nine months, small-town gossip starts to take its toll on him. Stephen Frears' comedy drama, based on Roddy Doyle's novel, starring Tina Kellegher and Colm Meaney

programme.title: The Pembrokeshire Murders
programme.desc: A long-awaited forensic breakthrough gives Detective Steve Wilkins and his team the ammunition they need to finally arrest and prosecute John Cooper. Drama based on the true story of John Cooper, a serial killer who evaded justice for more than 20 years. Starring Luke Evans and Keith Allen

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Julia Roberts talks about her latest role in psychological campus drama After the Hunt. Colin Farrell discusses playing a con man and gambling addict in atmospheric thriller Ballad of a Small Player. Singer Gloria Estefan introduces her new Spanish-language album Raices. And Benedict Cumberbatch talks about playing a newly widowed father in The Thing with Feathers. Plus, Robbie Williams performs Pretty Face from the forthcoming album BRITPOP

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi comes to the aid of John and Ilona, who bought the 10-bedroom Yeoldon House Hotel in Bideford, Devon as a retirement project. Despite having no industry experience, they ploughed their savings into the project. However, the reality has been far from the good life, with out-of-season occupancy below 10 per cent and the pair unable to afford a salary

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Comedian Vic Reeves and his wife Nancy Sorrell put on a dinner party as they play host to four strangers, who have to try and work out their identities by solving cryptic clues. Hosted by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager sits down with Tommy Martin. In the first part, he discusses his playing days, including the glory years of Ireland's World Cup campaigns

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner make four mouth-watering dishes with only five main ingredients, starting with a garlic and rosemary focaccia, followed by John's pink spaghetti. Following that are chicken and peanut butter scissor-cut noodles and moreish and fuss-free banana doughnuts.

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follows the stories of two women looking for siblings given up for adoption, but for very different reasons. Jeanette Woodyatt wants to find the brother she has never met and who was turned away from the family by her grandmother, while Karen Turnbull yearns to let her twin siblings know that their mother gave them up for adoption so they would have a better life

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Cambridge for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Chester for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard asks the questions as Phil Tufnell, Colin Jackson and Lisa Riley answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Samia Longchambon, Amy Dowden and Will Best all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: Week In Review looks back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the fourth live semi-final as more acts that made it through the auditions perform in front of the judges and a live audience for a chance at a place in the final. Viewers hold the power to vote for their favourite acts of the night to send through to the ultimate stage of the competition, and one judge has the Golden Buzzer that can reward one outstanding performer with a ticket straight through

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Limitless Win
programme.desc: Contestants get the chance to win the biggest cash prize in British TV history with a limitless jackpot. First up are Joe and sister-in-law Jo, who have been prepping by going round the supermarket sizing things up with a tape measure, followed by Steve and son-in-law Jordan, who hope their approach will help propel them into the truly life-changing sums of money on the ladder

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Irish Road Trip
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp head to snowy Northern Ireland, diving into Selkie myths, vampire legends, the origins of hurling, haunted hotels and Derry's epic Halloween festival

programme.title: Protection
programme.desc: Police officer DI Liz Nyles works in the secretive and high-stakes world of Witness Protection. One morning, a devastating incident at work raises grave questions about the integrity of Liz's unit, which is rocked with accusations of corruption. Drama, starring Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Celebrity Health Stories
programme.desc: After sharing her excruciating painful period experiences with her 3.2 million Instagram followers, actress Jacqueline Jossa is on a personal journey to investigate the condition of painful periods with medical experts, and start a fresh conversation about periods
